title: The Eight Woes
subtitle: Christ pronounces eternal woe upon the hypocrites who shut heaven against men

stillness: Be still, and let your prayer be short before God,
for it is not the long word that is heard,
but the affection of the heart.
gospel: Woe to you, Scribes and Pharisees, hypocrites,
who shut the kingdom of heaven against men;
for you yourselves do not enter,
and those that are entering, you do not suffer to enter.
Woe to you, who devour the substance of the poor,
the houses of widows, under a long pretence of prayer:
for this you shall receive the greater judgement.
Woe to you, who compass sea and land to make one proselyte;
and when he is made, you make him a son of hell,
behold: After He had given His blessings to those who keep the Law,
the Lord threatens the disobedient Scribes and Pharisees
with the woe of His malediction.
He sets down eight woes against the hypocrites
who falsely counterfeit righteousness,
to show what in them must be shunned.
Woe — that is, eternal damnation — hangs over you,
you Scribes, who think yourselves learned in the Law,
and you Pharisees, who call yourselves chief in holiness,
hypocrites, feigning what you are not.
You shut the kingdom of heaven before men,
for by your crooked expositions you hide the truth of the Scriptures,
and by your evil example you scandalize the rest.
You yourselves do not enter, because you will not believe;
and those who long to believe, you do not suffer to enter,
but rather you make them go astray.
Under the show of divine worship you pray long prayers,
long in words, but short in feeling and in meaning,
that you may seem holy and receive the greater gifts;
and you devour the substance of the poor,
the houses of widows, who are more easily deceived,
having no husband to counsel them.
You compass sea and land for a single proselyte,
not out of mercy, to save him,
but out of vainglory, or out of avarice;
and when he is made, you make him a son of hell,
twofold more than yourselves.
consider: Woe in Holy Scripture marks eternal damnation and the punishment of hell.
The kingdom of heaven is the Sacred Scriptures, in which heaven lies hidden.
He who carries the key and does not open shuts that kingdom against another:
he who has knowledge, yet scorns or neglects to teach.
And he who, by evil example, hinders others from entering, sets up an obstacle.
First they had the knowledge of Christ;
but afterward, blinded by envy, they lost it,
and were turned into error, and made many others to err.
These hypocrites are damned in a twofold way:
for their hidden iniquity, because they are wicked;
and for their open pretence, because they show what they are not.
Feigned righteousness is not righteousness, but a double iniquity;
and therefore, when the recompense comes, it must be struck with the greater punishment.
He who does evil is worthy of punishment;
but he who takes from religion the occasion of his malice is bound to a heavier one.
Their very prayer is turned into sin:
not only can they not intercede for others,
they cannot profit even themselves unto salvation;
nay, for those very prayers they are the more damned.
Their labour is empty, for many of them compass sea and land for one;
but Christ, alone, goes through the cities and villages, and great crowds follow Him.
And their wickedness is laid bare:
the man they labour to win, they make worse than themselves,
a son of hell, a son of perdition, twofold.
prayer: who give blessings to those who obey your Law and your precepts,
grant me of your bounty always to obey those precepts of yours,
and in all things to shun the errors of the hypocrites.
Grant me to rest in your warnings and your good pleasure without weariness,
and to amend myself without negligence;
that at last, at the coming of your brightness unto judgement,
I may look with gladness upon you, the Blessed One,
coming in open manifestation unto our full redemption,
the perfect freedom of body and soul,
and with your elect may bless you without end. Amen.

latinJewel.latin: Simulata æquitas non est æquitas, sed duplex iniquitas.
latinJewel.english: Feigned righteousness is not righteousness, but a double iniquity.
